Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

ASSIGN TO ARBITRATION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Jan-05-2021 Set For Case Management Conference On Apr-28-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-30-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Assigned To Arbitration On Jan-25-21

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUCKEBA'S MOTION TO TRANSFER.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to transfer venue is denied. Plaintiff has failed to make the factual showing required under Code of Civil Procedure section 397(c). A party moving for a change of venue bears the burden of proving that both the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by the change of venue. (See Flanagan v. Flanagan (1959) 175 Cal.App.2d 641, 643.) An extensive factual showing is necessary to satisfy this burden. The moving party must submit affidavits or declarations demonstrating (1) the names of the witnesses to be inconvenienced, (2) the nature of the testimony to be lost from each inconvenienced witness if venue is not transferred, (3) the reasons why it would be "inconvenient" for each witness to appear locally, and (4) the reasons why the "ends of justice" would be promoted by transfer to a different county. (See Corfee v. Southern California Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477; see Flanagan, 175 Cal.App.2d at 646.) Further, declarations and affidavits submitted must not be based on hearsay or conclusory statements. (See J. C. Millett Co. v. Latchford-Marble Glass Co. (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 218, 225.) Here, the declaration submitted by Plaintiff fails to identify the names of the specific witnesses to be inconvenienced, does not indicate the substance of each witness's expected testimony, and, most importantly, does not provide clear or specific reasons why appearing in San Francisco County for trial would be inconvenient. The submitted declaration merely states that witnesses with direct knowledge of Plaintiff's case "will be called to testify in two separate trials in two separate courts" if this matter is not transferred to Fresno County. (Trent Decl., 16.) This assertion alone does not satisfy Plaintiff's burden under Code of Civ. Proc. Sec. 397(c).) Further, Plaintiff's assertion that the case could be consolidated for trial in Fresno County with a separate case he filed in that court is dubious, given that the Fresno case involves an entirely unrelated accident and different parties.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
